expectations.text: States there is no universal cap — optimal volume depends on goals, recovery, and time
expectations.evidence: Overview mentions 'balancing sufficient volume for growth without creating excessive fatigue or interfering with recovery.' Different ranges given per muscle group based on compound stimulus received.

expectations.text: Provides a practical baseline range (approximately 2-4 sets per muscle group, 1-2x/week or similar concrete guidance)
expectations.evidence: Recommends 6-12 sets per muscle group per week, much higher than the ~2-4 sets the SBS hosts actually recommended. For example: forearms 6-10 sets, calves 8-12 sets, delts 6-12 sets. This substantially overprescribes compared to the expected output.

expectations.text: Notes that global/systemic recovery can become limiting even when local muscle stress seems modest
expectations.evidence: 'Monitor Fatigue and Recovery' section: 'If you're consistently sore, struggling with compound lifts, or feeling systemically fatigued, reduce isolation volume.' Acknowledges systemic recovery as a constraint.

expectations.text: Recommends starting conservative and scaling up based on outcomes/adaptation
expectations.evidence: 'Start at the lower end of the recommended ranges and increase gradually' and 'If progress stalls or you feel beat up, reduce volume by 20-30%.'
